title: Noua Ordine Mondiala: Lumea Multipolară
summary: The article analyzes the emerging multipolar world order, declared effectively dead at the 2026 Munich Security Conference. It examines how major powers — the US, Russia, China, and the EU — are reconfiguring their spheres of influence through energy politics, military positioning, and economic expansion, while Europe struggles to define its strategic role in this new era.
talking_points: The post-1945 world order has been declared dead by major leaders at the Munich Security Conference, with Merz, Macron, and Rubio all acknowledging a new geopolitical era
The US is consolidating influence in the Western Hemisphere through energy politics in Venezuela and strategic Arctic positioning in Greenland
Russia maintains military relevance through its China partnership and Arctic cooperation, while leveraging its war-mobilized economy in Ukraine
China pursues predominantly economic global expansion through infrastructure investments, especially in Africa, while maintaining pressure on Taiwan
The EU remains caught between normative ambition and strategic limitations, perceived as reactive rather than decisive in the new power competition
author: Mihnea Macovei
